title: The Royal Fork
setup: Black's king on c6 and queen on d5 sit just one knight-leap apart — the classic family-fork distance.
hint: Find the knight square that hits **both** the king and the queen at once. The leap to {{b4}} touches c6 and d5.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the **royal fork** — a knight check that simultaneously attacks the queen. Black's king and queen are exactly a knight-leap apart: from {{b4}} the knight reaches c6 (the king) and d5 (the queen) on the same move.

After {{Nb4+}} Black has no good answer:

- **Capture?** The king on c6 cannot reach b4 (two squares away), and the queen on d5 cannot reach b4 either — d5 to b4 is a knight pattern, not a queen move.
- **Block?** A knight check can never be blocked.
- **Run?** Every legal king square (Kb5, Kb6, Kc7, Kd6, Kd7) abandons the queen.

So Black plays {{Kb6}} (or any king move) and White scoops the queen with {{Nxd5}}.

## Pattern to Memorize

> Whenever the enemy king and queen are exactly **two squares apart on a diagonal**, look for the knight square between them — that's where the fork lives.
